subject: Project Management
title: Combining Lean and Six Sigma
description: A quick win: understand Combining Lean and Six Sigma.

hook.text: Lean eliminates waste. Six Sigma eliminates variation. Together, they're unstoppable.

buildup.text: Lean makes processes faster. Six Sigma makes them more precise. The combination delivers both speed and quality.

discovery.text: Lean Six Sigma emerged in the early 2000s as companies realized neither method alone was enough.

twist.text: Purists on both sides resisted the merger. Lean advocates called Six Sigma bloated; sigma fans called lean simplistic.

climax.text: Lean Six Sigma became the most widely adopted improvement methodology in the corporate world.

punchline.text: Speed meets precision. That's the winning formula.

quiz.question: What does Lean Six Sigma combine?
options: Lean's waste elimination with Six Sigma's variation reduction
